events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need decide fail because DP impulse lines sloped wrong for service: gas taps below pipe / liquid taps on top. This is classic non-compliant for differential pressure flow measurement. API RP 551 and manufacturer manuals require impulse lines slope to avoid gas pockets in liquid service and liquid traps in gas/vapor service. For gas service, taps should be on top/side and lines slope back to pipe or have drains? For liquid, taps on side/bottom and lines slope up to transmitter or have gas vents. Wrong slope leads to measurement error, potential liquid lock in gas lines or gas bubbles in liquid lines, inaccurate control, safety. Work rough-complete. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Risk high? Could cause process control issues, but not immediate catastrophic. In commercial project, high due to measurement error and potential safety. Decision fail. Need include findings, actions, rationale, references. percent_complete maybe 70? rough-complete. value not needed 0. sound etc default. order empty, workable empty. Need valid JSON. Ensure no prose outside.

    "Gas service impulse taps/lines are routed below the pipe, creating liquid traps.",
    "Liquid service impulse taps/lines are routed on top of the pipe, creating gas pockets.",
    "Incorrect slope will cause measurement error, unstable control, and possible transmitter damage."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove and re-run impulse lines with correct slope per service.",
    "For gas/vapor service, locate taps on top or side and slope lines back toward the process or provide drains where needed.",
    "For liquid service, locate taps on the side or bottom and slope lines up toward the transmitter or provide vents where needed.",
    "Re-inspect before close-out and functional testing."
  ],
  "rationale": "API RP 551 and transmitter manufacturer instructions require impulse lines to be arranged so gas lines do not trap liquid and liquid lines do not trap gas. The observed routing is the classic reverse arrangement, making the installation defective and non-compliant.",

    "API RP 551 - Process Control Instrumentation",
    "Manufacturer transmitter installation manuals"
  ]
}
